rationale: The citing paper cites De Jong et al. (2008) not just as background, but as a specific prior approach in response-style research that its own method is contrasted against. In its contribution statements, the paper explicitly says that unlike work such as De Jong et al., it does not restrict analysis to predefined prominent styles and instead identifies potentially unknown response styles while separating style from item content. This shows the cited paper helps define the methodological limitation the new LC-BML model is designed to address.
relation_description: The citing paper positions its LC-BML model as a methodological refinement relative to De Jong et al. (2008), relaxing a narrower predefined response-style setup by allowing broader, latent discovery of response styles and joint modeling with item content.
